notes: No test-inventory.json present — every file is effectively depth 0 (no tests exist). business_critical applied via domain reasoning: tool mutates/deletes files on disk.

areas.summary: Highest-churn file (9 commits). Owns safeMove, moveSidecarFiles, pruneEmptyDirectories, removeKnownQueueArtifacts — the only code that mutates/deletes the user's filesystem. A bug here loses data.
524-line pure parser (parseMediaFile). Wrong parse => wrong destination. High churn (8), zero deps to mock => ideal first target.
constructMoviePath / constructTvShowPath / constructTvShowDatePath. Pure path construction; wrong output => misfiled media. Easy to assert, high value.
639-line TMDbClient (findBestMovieMatch, findBestTvMatch, getEpisodeInfo, getEpisodeByAirDate). External API + best-match heuristics. Mock axios; wrong match => wrong metadata.
High churn (7) and the most-depended-on module (env feeds logger, fs, tmdb, formatter). Validates TMDB_API_KEY / paths; misconfig fails everything downstream.

below_cut.note: Orchestrator; hard to unit test (class + heavy I/O). Cover via integration after core units exist.
Largest file (766 LOC); same integration-test posture as cli-movies.
Types only; no runtime behavior to test.
